title: cuscuta globulosa
intro: Cuscuta globulosa, also known as the yellow dodder, is a parasitic plant that belongs to the family Convolvulaceae, obtaining its nutrients by attaching itself to other plants and siphoning their nutrients. Traditionally, Cuscuta globulosa has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral activities. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and possessing immunomodulatory effects. Cuscuta globulosa can be prepared into herbal supplements, teas, or tinctures, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine.
